title: Skippy the Jedi Droid

"'''Skippy the Jedi Droid'''" is an eight-page [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] story featured in [[Star Wars Tales 1|''Star Wars Tales'' 1]], written by [[Peter David]]. It takes place shortly before and during [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|Episode IV]], and features [[R5-D4/Legends|R5-D4]] as "Skippy".

==Plot summary==
[[File:Skipp_the_droid.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Skippy, the Jedi droid.]]

[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] leaves a [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]], sensing [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] in someone. But no matter how hard he looked, he wasn't able to find anyone, despite his great ability in the Force. Suddenly, unbeknownst to Obi-Wan, an [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] serving drinks at [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|Jabba's Palace]] is bumped into by [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], spilling one of the drinks from his tray. But before it can hit the ground, the droid, named [[R5-D4/Legends|R5-D4]], is able to reach out with his mind and turn the drink back in its upright position and serve it to [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]]; thankfully nobody had noticed what had just happened, as it had gone by so fast.

One day, R5 removed his restraining bolt with the Force, and [[Mind trick/Legends|Force persuaded]] the [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] guards to let him leave. Skippy wandered the desert for days in search of his destiny, until finally he was picked up by a [[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]] [[Sandcrawler/Legends|sandcrawler]]. There he had visions of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] and [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]], and met [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]]. Eventually the Sandcrawler stopped, took all the droids out, and lined them up side by side in front of [[Owen Lars/Legends|Owen Lars]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. Skippy immediately sensed the Force in Luke, and persuaded Owen to choose him. Skippy was ecstatic at the undoubtable destiny that lay before him and Luke.

However, R5 soon envisioned that, without Luke, [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] would come and take R2 back to Vader, where he would be destroyed and Leia would be killed, leading to a chain reaction of horror that will include the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]] being destroyed, R5's memory being wiped after 3PO refuses to translate his 'rubbish', and Luke dying on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] without ever fulfilling his destiny. R5 knew what he had to do, and suddenly set off a mild explosion inside himself, leading Luke to believe that he had a busted motivator. Skippy then used the Force on 3PO, telling Luke to buy R2 instead. Owen did so, leaving R5 to be destroyed by a stray stormtrooper blast during the later raid, with nobody ever knowing the great deed that R5 had just done.

==Continuity==
Peter David originally wrote "Skippy the Jedi Droid" as a humor text story for his column "But I Digress" in the then-weekly comics magazine ''Comics Buyer's Guide''.  It was originally published in ''Comics Buyer's Guide'' #1219 on March 28, 1997.<ref>https://www.peterdavid.net/2012/05/07/skippy-the-jedi-droid/</ref> While most of the text was adapted into the comic, some parts were removed:
*a remark from the narrator about droids being unable to send e-mail or copy a file, which was a reference to David's criticism in ''Comics Buyer's Guide'' #1218 (hence why he remarked "we covered it last week") about how using e-mail and copying the file of the Death Star Plans could've solved the Rebellion's problems.
*A segment in which Skippy learned from other Droids about Jedi Knights and researched them, and then later when Skippy is unable to prove to the other Droids that he had Force abilities.
*Skippy hiding from the Sandworms from [[Wikipedia:Dune_(franchise)|''Dune'']].

As the column was originally published in 1997, years before the release of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''The Phantom Menace'']], the explanation that Skippy had lubricants rife with [[Midi-chlorian|"Midi-chloroxians"]] was added in later.

In the in-universe article ''[[Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena]]'', the legend of Skippy the Jedi Droid is mentioned. This means that while this Tales story is not wholly canon, the legend of Skippy does exist.

Skippy also appears as a mascot for teaching [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]], much like a cartoon character would impart important life lessons, in the also non-canonical story ''[[Tag & Bink: Revenge of the Clone Menace]]''.

Droids bearing resemblance to [[Wikipedia:Tom Servo|Tom Servo]] and [[Wikipedia:Crow T. Robot|Crow T. Robot]] of [[Wikipedia:Mystery Science Theater 3000|''Mystery Science Theater 3000'']] and [[Wikipedia:Bender Bending Rodríguez|Bender]] of [[Wikipedia:Futurama|''Futurama'']] make appearances among the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] who make fun of Skippy. (If his appearance in the fan film ''TROOPS'', and Star Wars: Chewbacca are counted, this is Tom Servo's third ''[[Star Wars]]'' appearance; they both also re-appear, with Skippy, in ''[[Tag & Bink: Revenge of the Clone Menace]]'') The Robinsons' robot from the series [[Wikipedia:Lost in Space|''Lost in Space'']] also makes an appearance later on (he previously appeared in ''[[Zorba the Hutt's Revenge]]'').

A tribute to [[Wikipedia:Rudolph the Red-Nosed Reindeer|''Rudolph the Red-Nosed Reindeer'']] is given when the narration states, "All of the other droids used to laugh and call him names. They never let poor Skippy join in any droid-type games."

Skippy also used the phrase "I'm not the droid you're looking for," a tribute to Obi-Wan Kenobi's line in ANH.

Skippy's changing vision of the Stormtroopers riding on [[Dewback]]s or a replica of one is a reference to the changes made in the then-recent [[The Star Wars Trilogy Special Edition|Special Edition]] of ''A New Hope.''

In one scene it shows an [[IG-series/Legends|IG-series]] droid with different hands.

[[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]] are referred to as the sand people from Tusken, but Tusken Raiders are really named after [[Fort Tusken/Legends|Fort Tusken]] which they invaded, not for their point of origin.  However, this may have originally been a joke in the original text, as it was in the middle of a paragraph critical of how there shouldn't be planets orbiting binary stars, and that Skippy was also hiding from the Sandworms from ''Dune''.<ref>https://www.peterdavid.net/2012/05/07/skippy-the-jedi-droid/</ref>
